title: Python link_layer.py: Domain separation vulnerability - _build_signable_data lacks dst_addr length prefix
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py
Lines: 239-244

The _build_signable_data method constructs the signed message as:

    return (
        bytes([epoch])
        + seqnum.to_bytes(2, "big")
        + dst_addr
        + payload
    )

Without a length prefix for dst_addr, the same hash is produced for different (dst_addr, payload) combinations when addr_mode changes:

- Frame A: addr_mode=EUI64, dst_addr=0x0102030405060708, payload=0x0A0B
- Frame B: addr_mode=BROADCAST, payload=0x01020304050607080A0B

Both produce identical signed messages: epoch || seqnum || 0x0102030405060708 || 0x0A0B

This is the same vulnerability as project-LICHEN-917q (Rust) and project-LICHEN-j7rk (C).

Fix: Add len(dst_addr) as a prefix byte:
    return (
        bytes([epoch])
        + seqnum.to_bytes(2, "big")
        + bytes([len(dst_addr)])
        + dst_addr
        + payload
    )
close_reason: Added destination-address length prefix to Python signable data, updated valid signature fixture, and verified focused tests plus Ruff.
